perturbation.original_text: IAC shall comply with all applicable laws and regulations related to the manufacturing and/or production of the Products in jurisdictions in which IAC manufactures the Products, including labor and employment, environmental, safety, tax and other similar regulations. In addition, IAC currently complies and will continue to comply with any applicable regulations regarding foreign child labor laws and other abusive labor practices.
perturbation.changed_text: IAC shall endeavor to comply with applicable laws and regulations related to the manufacturing and/or production of the Products in jurisdictions in which IAC manufactures the Products, including labor and employment, environmental, safety, tax and other similar regulations. Additionally, IAC will strive to comply with regulations regarding foreign child labor laws and other abusive labor practices, as reasonably practical.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall comply' to 'shall endeavor to comply' introduces ambiguity, making compliance discretionary rather than mandatory. Adding 'as reasonably practical' further weakens the obligation concerning child labor laws. This creates a contradiction by making it difficult to enforce strict compliance with labor laws.
perturbation.law_explanation: The original text mandates full compliance, while the modified text allows for partial or discretionary compliance. The Fair Labor Standards Act requires strict adherence to labor laws, particularly regarding child labor. The phrase 'as reasonably practical' could be interpreted to justify non-compliance under certain circumstances, directly conflicting with the FLSA's intent to prevent exploitative labor practices.

The Hours Worked Advisor provides information to help determine which hours spent in work-related activities are considered FLSA “hours worked” and therefore must be paid. The Overtime Security Advisor helps determine which employees are exempt from the FLSA minimum wage and overtime pay requirements under the Part 541 overtime regulations. The Overtime Calculator Advisor computes the amount of overtime pay due in a sample pay period based on information from the user. The Child Labor Rules Advisor answers questions about the FLSA’s youth employment provisions, including at what age young people can work and the jobs they can perform. The Section 14(c) Advisor helps users understand the special minimum wage requirements for workers with disabilities. perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces ambiguity and weakens the obligation to comply with labor laws, particularly concerning child labor. By changing 'shall comply' to 'shall endeavor to comply' and adding 'as reasonably practical,' the contract now allows for discretionary compliance, conflicting with the strict adherence expected by labor laws. Scraped Law Snippet 1, which discusses civil money penalty inflation adjustments, outlines penalties for violations of child labor standards under 29 USC 216(e)(1)(A)(i) and 29 CFR 570.140(b)(1). The modification creates a legal contradiction by making it possible to justify non-compliance under the guise of 'reasonable practicality,' thereby potentially violating child labor laws.

perturbation.original_text: IAC shall maintain various quality control programs consistent with best practices for the industry, each of which will be provided to Sonos if requested. When applicable, any additional or substitute quality requirements agreed to by the Parties shall be made to such programs and plans.
perturbation.changed_text: IAC will seek to maintain quality control programs that it believes are generally in line with industry practices, documentation of which may be provided to Sonos upon reasonable request. Any additional or substitute quality requirements agreed to by the Parties might be incorporated into such programs and plans.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing 'shall maintain' with 'will seek to maintain', the obligation to have concrete quality control programs becomes discretionary. Replacing 'consistent with best practices' with 'that it believes are generally in line' introduces a subjective element, weakening the requirement. Additionally, 'documentation of which may be provided' makes providing documentation optional. Finally, 'might be incorporated' reduces the obligation to include agreed upon quality requirements. This creates ambiguity and makes enforcement difficult.
perturbation.law_explanation: 21 CFR Part 820.20 mandates that medical device manufacturers establish and maintain a quality system that includes documented procedures. The modified text introduces doubt and optionality, suggesting that such systems might not be fully maintained or documented, creating a contradiction of this FDA regulation if the manufactured product is a medical device.

perturbation.justification: The modified text significantly weakens the obligation to maintain quality control programs, introducing ambiguity and optionality. The original text requires IAC to 'maintain' quality control programs, while the changed text states IAC 'will seek to maintain' programs. Additionally, documentation is made optional ('may be provided'). According to 21 CFR Part 820.20, medical device manufacturers must 'establish and maintain a quality system'. The changes in the contract text create a contradiction because they suggest the systems 'might not be fully maintained or documented', as the Law Explanation states, if the manufactured product is a medical device, making it non-compliant with 21 CFR Part 820. The first scraped law snippet references 21 CFR Part 820, which mandates a quality system.
